title: Tigger's Quest for the Wizard's Magic Wand
summary: Tigger and Christopher Robin set out on an adventure to reach the Wizard’s castle, hoping to receive a magic wand. Along the way, they overcome a towering wall with a clever stack of items and brave a dark, friendly forest inhabited by singing birds and curious insects. Upon arriving at the magnificent stone castle, they are greeted by a Queen and reunite with their friend Eeyore. They climb a long, winding staircase to meet Peacock the Wizard—a pink and yellow bird—who teaches them the art of rhyming magic. Entrusted with a magical wand, the group prepares for a new quest to find the Wizard's lost hat before heading back into the night.
audioFilename: Stars and stories - journey to the wizard’s castle 2.m4a

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's the stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now I have to...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And I'll turn out the light.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and stories starting. I'm a little bit of stories and this is my own spot in the shelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, Izzy's own spot in the shelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now, who should we tell a story about tonight?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Eeyore. No, maybe Tigger.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a story about Tigger. Once upon a time, Tigger was bouncing along down the road. And along the road, coming from the other direction was someone surprising. Who did he bump into?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: His friend Christopher Robin.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, Tigger bumped into his friend Christopher Robin. And Christopher Robin said, "Tigger, listen. I'm on an adventure. Will you come with me?" And what did Tigger say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would sure!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Christopher Robin said, "I'm on an adventure to go to the Wizard's castle. And it's going to be a long journey to get there. But once we get there, maybe the wizard will give us a magic wand." And what did Tigger say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, maybe he won't and maybe he will.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Maybe he won't and maybe he will, and that's the way it is with adventures," said Tigger. And Christopher Robin said, "That's quite right. A healthy attitude, I think. You never can tell with wizards. Now, we've got to go a long way down this road until we get to a dead end. And at that point, we'll have to climb over a wall and go across a field. The castle's a long way after that, but that'll give us a good start." And so they walked all the way down to the end of the road and they tried to climb the wall, but it was too high. So what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They tried a stool!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right! Tigger said, "Oh no, even I can't jump high enough to jump over this wall. We'd better find something to help us." And Christopher Robin said...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Christopher Robin said, "Let me run back to my house. Wait there." And Tigger said, "Okay, I'll wait right there." And and Christopher Robin said, "I'm going to get a stool." And Tigger said, "Okay, I'll wait. Boing boing boing boing boing."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Christopher Robin went to his own house and got a stool. Yeah, and then he ran back, and then he ran on back to Tigger.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tigger tried to jump up off the wall, but it was too high. So what did he do next? He put so much things on top of the stool.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and then he climbed all the way on top of the stack of things on the stool and jumped from there. And did it work this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes! It worked! Tigger jumped all the way up onto the wall and then jumped down the other side and he said, "Hooray, I made it!" And and Tigger said, "Christopher Robin, can you try my stack of things?"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Christopher Robin said, "Well, all right, I'll try it. I'm a little nervous. Um, if you sing me a song to help me, maybe I'll be more brave. Will you sing me a song, Tigger?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I will.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, can you sing it, please?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe Jingle Bells?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes, I love that song.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, and why are you not telling the story?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I was waiting for Jingle Bells, said Christopher Robin. I'm waiting for my song to help me be brave. Will you sing it for me, please, Tigger?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I will. Okay, go ahead. Jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle all the way. Oh what fun it is to ride in a one-horse open sleigh. Oh jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle all the way. Oh what fun it is to ride in a one-horse open sleigh. Oh jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle all the way. Oh what fun it is to ride in a one-horse open sleigh. Oh jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle bells, jingle all the way. Oh what fun it is to ride in a one-horse open sleigh. Oh jingle bells, jingle bells...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Christopher Robin jumped down with a thump on the other side of the wall right next to Tigger who was still singing happily. And Christopher Robin said, "Well, thank you for singing that brave song. It really helped me be brave and I climbed up on the stool and onto the big pile of things and I hopped right over the wall very easily with your help. Thank you, Tigger." And what did Tigger say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you for trying out my stack of things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And thank you for building it for us. Now, let's continue on on our adventure. From here, we have to walk on a path through the dark forest. And then once we come out the other side of the forest, at the top of a hill, we'll see it: the Wizard's castle. And so they entered the forest on the dark winding path and they walked along quietly in the cool light of the shade of the trees. And they heard birds chirping quietly in the trees - chirp, chirp, chirp - and they saw the sunlight filtering down between the leaves and their feet went crunch, crunch, crunch on the leaves and twigs as they walked along the path. And what else did they see in the forest?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They saw twigs and worms and slugs and bugs!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they said, "Oh, hello twigs, hello worms, hello slugs, hello bugs." For everyone in the forest was friendly. And so Christopher Robin became calm and happy as they walked through the forest. And Tigger started to hum to himself and boing. And he sang a happy little humming boinging song. "Forest, forest, I love the forest. Forest, forest, I love it here." And Christopher Robin hummed along. And so in this way, they made their way along the path through the forest, in one side, all the way across, and out the other end. And when they came out of the forest back into the bright sunny day, they looked ahead of them and saw a hill. And you know what was at the top of the hill?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Wizard's castle!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The castle was a beautiful tall gray stone building with tall turrets, which are a kind of skinny tower with a pointy roof on top, and there were little flags on top of the pointy roofs and there was one very big wooden door right in the center of the castle. It was a friendly looking castle. And it had a path leading right up to the door. And what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They knocked on the door and the Queen said, "Hello everyone! Thanks for inviting you both."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Christopher Robin and Tigger said, "Oh, thanks for inviting us here. May we come in?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You may.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. And so the Queen led them inside. And what did they see inside the castle?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They saw so many things they can do.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like the toys or snacks, or do some office time with the Queen. And and their friend Eeyore said, "I came here too!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, Eeyore came here too. And so what did Tigger and Christopher Robin decide to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They decided to go and play in the office.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the office was up a very long staircase that wound around and around and around. They said, "Puff, puff, puff, our legs are getting tired." And the Queen said, "Oh, it's just a few more stairs higher, keep going." And Christopher Robin and Tigger climbed and climbed all those stairs...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Eeyore!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: ...and Eeyore, until they got all the way to the top of one of the towers, which is where the office was. And you know whose office it was?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes! It was the Wizard's office.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the Wizard had a great many toys. And who is the Wizard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Wizard is a magical creature who happens to also be an animal.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of animal was the Wizard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a kind of bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The Wizard was a bird. What color bird?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A pink and yellow bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The Wizard was a pink and yellow bird who could do wonderful magic. And what was the Wizard's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Peacock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Peacock the Wizard, a pink and yellow bird who wore a funny little hat. What color was the hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Birds don't wear hats!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right. Birds don't wear hats. Well, Peacock the Wizard saw his door opening and in came Christopher Robin and Tigger...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: ...and Eeyore!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: ...and Eeyore. And he said, "Welcome to my office everyone, I'm Peacock the Wizard. You're right on time."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Wizard, you don't have a hat. You don't have a hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Peacock the Wizard said, "That's right, I may be a bird and that means I don't wear a hat. But because I am a wizard bird, that means if I want to I could. And do you know what?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Wizard, I'm a person and I wear hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Ah, so I see. Christopher Robin, you are a person. Now..."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And me too, I am a person too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "As I was saying," said Peacock the Wizard bird, "Once upon a time I used to have a hat, but oh dear me, I've lost it. I wonder if you could help me with this problem."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We can! Me too!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Well, if you're willing to go on an adventure to help me find my lost hat, I could help you by giving you a tool to use along the way."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mickey! Mickey can help me! Mickey, Mickey!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Who's Mickey?" says Peacock.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mickey is a stuffed Disney, he and he is a wonderful he can also do magic tricks, like this: my wand in my hand! Hello everyone, I can do magic tricks!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Wow," said Peacock the Wizard bird. "Mickey can do magic tricks too. Now, perhaps all of you could help me find my lost hat."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What, an old hat or a new hat?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "It's an old hat."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh Mickey, I better find you I better find a new hat for you, Bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, well thank you. Now, if you're willing to go on this quest, I will give to you a magic wand that will allow you to do magic. Do you accept this special tool?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "And let me show you how to use it."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Show me, show me!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "All you have to do is make up a rhyme. It has to be at least two words that rhyme with each other. They don't have to be real words even. And then you say your rhyme and you wave the magic wand in the air and it makes a funny sound, and then you can do your magic, such as transforming one thing into another. So for instance, hmm, let's take this chair right here. I will wave my magic wand and say, hmm, let's make up a rhyme. Can you make up a rhyme?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Junk and gunk!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Junk and gunk! Brring! And then the chair transforms into a bucket. Well, that's what the magic wand lets you do. Suppose I wanted to change the bucket back into a chair, I would simply have to come up with a new rhyme and transform it. You see? That's the magic wand. Now, here you are. And who would like to take it?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Me me me! And I want one too!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Okay, well I have just the one so I'll entrust it for now to Tigger or to Christopher Robin, or perhaps to Mickey or Eeyore."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or maybe me!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, Izzy the kiddo can take the magic wand to go on the quest. And now, I must say goodbye."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Why?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Because it is time for me to go to sleep," said Peacock the Wizard bird. "Goodnight." And so Peacock the Wizard bird got in his bed to go to sleep and the party of adventurers went down the stairs and out the door of the castle to begin their next adventure. And that is the end of tonight's story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what's next? Dragon next!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, let's turn off the stars and turn on the light.

characters.name: Tigger
characters.description: A friendly and boisterous Super Sleuth from the Hundred Acre Wood, this bouncing adventurer zips into action on a scooter, bringing a whirlwind of enthusiasm to help his friends and aid the local mice.

characters.name: Christopher Robin
characters.description: The master of the house at the final destination is a gentle boy and companion to Tigger, recently returned from a fishing trip to serve as the catalyst for the pilgrimage to the Wizard’s castle. Navigating the halls in a small car, he wanders the gallery of existence with a quiet, unsettling grace, pausing as a helpful friend to assist in inflating balloons. A reverent guest in his own domain, he visits Piglet—the foundational architect of a looming "big bad"—before setting the gears of the quest in motion and retiring home for a nap.

characters.name: Peacock
characters.description: Residing within the loftiest tower of a hilltop castle, this majestic bird wizard is draped in a vibrant mantle of pink and yellow feathers that shimmer with arcane grace.

characters.name: The Queen
characters.description: A castle dweller who welcomes the travelers and leads them to the office.

characters.name: Eeyore
characters.description: A somber donkey and companion to Tigger and Christopher Robin within the castle walls, he celebrates his birthday under a trademark cloud of gloom, certain that his friends have arrived empty-handed and forgotten his special day.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

places.name: The Road
places.description: Where Tigger and Christopher Robin first meet to start their quest.

places.name: The Wall
places.description: A high obstacle that the adventurers overcome using a stool and a stack of items.

places.name: The Dark Forest
places.description: A friendly forest filled with twigs, worms, slugs, and bugs.

places.name: The Wizard's Castle
places.description: Crowned upon a hill, the gray stone castle is distinguished by its tall turrets and a soaring tower, the lofty home where Peacock the Wizard resides behind a beautiful wooden door.

places.name: The Wizard's Office
places.description: Located at the top of a winding staircase in a castle tower.
